    created_date: model.created_date,
    modified_date: model.modified_date
  };
}

🔎 Why this works

  • Destructuring: pulls only the fields you want from PurchaseOrderModel.

  • Nullish coalescing (??): uses document_number if order_no is missing.

  • Voucher date: prefers voucherDate if present, otherwise voucher_date.

  • Conditional user assignment: sets created_by or modified_by depending on btnType.

  • Approval status: hardcoded to "Pending".

⚡ This way you ignore extra parameters like voucher_id, register_name, account_name, etc., and only map what PurchaseOrderModelSave actually needs.

Would you like me to also show you a generic utility function (like pick<T>(obj, keys)) so you can reuse this mapping pattern anywhere without rewriting destructuring each time?

You said
this.AssignValues(this.purchaseOrderModel); the value fin the model is passing here
Copilot said

Right, Mumthas 👌 — when you call:

ts
this.AssignValues(this.purchaseOrderModel);

you’re simply passing the current instance of purchaseOrderModel (which is of type PurchaseOrderModel) into your AssignValues method. Inside that method, you then map its values into purchaseOrderModelSave.

🔎 What happens step by step

  1. Call site

    • this.purchaseOrderModel already holds values (maybe from a form or API).

    • You pass it into AssignValues.

  2. Method signature

    ts
    AssignValues(model: PurchaseOrderModel) {
  // model here is exactly this.purchaseOrderModel
}

  3. Inside the method

    • You copy selected properties from model into this.purchaseOrderModelSave.

    • Example:

      ts
      this.purchaseOrderModelSave.register_code = model.register_code;
this.purchaseOrderModelSave.account_code = model.account_code;

    • You also handle special cases (like order_no fallback, created_by vs modified_by).

  4. Result

    • After the call, purchaseOrderModelSave is populated with the values from purchaseOrderModel.

    • Any extra fields in PurchaseOrderModel that aren’t needed are ignored.

✅ Simplified version

If you want to keep it short and clear:

ts
AssignValues(model: PurchaseOrderModel) {
  this.purchaseOrderModelSave = {
    ...this.purchaseOrderModelSave,
    register_code: model.register_code,
    account_code: model.account_code,
    document_number: model.document_number,
    order_no: model.order_no ?? model.document_number,
    voucher_date: model.voucherDate ?? model.voucher_date,
    request_no: model.request_no,
    payterm_id: model.payterm_id,
    mode_shipment: model.mode_shipment,
    voucher_reference: model.voucher_reference,
    narration: model.narration,
    approval_status: 'Pending',
    approver_remarks: model.approver_remarks,
    cur_no: model.cur_no,
    foreign_line_amount: model.foreign_line_amount,
    foreign_discount_amount: model.foreign_discount_amount,
    foreign_enter_amount: model.foreign_enter_amount,
    line_amount: model.line_amount,
    discount: model.discount,
    enter_amount: model.enter_amount,
    created_by: this.btnType === 'N' ? localStorage.getItem('user_id') : undefined,
    modified_by: this.btnType === 'M' ? localStorage.getItem('user_id') : undefined
  };
}

👉 So yes, when you call this.AssignValues(this.purchaseOrderModel);, the values inside purchaseOrderModel are passed into the method and copied into purchaseOrderModelSave.

Would you like me to also show you how to automatically ignore extra fields (so you don’t have to list them manually) using a utility like pick()?

You said
can you make it little more short
Copilot said

Absolutely Mumthas 👌 — here’s a super‑short version of your AssignValues method that still handles all the logic:

ts
AssignValues(model: PurchaseOrderModel) {
  const userId = localStorage.getItem('user_id');
  this.purchaseOrderModelSave = {
    ...model,
    voucher_date: model.voucherDate ?? model.voucher_date,
    approval_status: 'Pending',
    order_no: model.order_no ?? model.document_number,
    created_by: this.btnType === 'N' ? userId : undefined,
    modified_by: this.btnType === 'M' ? userId : undefined
  };
}

🔎 Why this is shorter

  • ...model copies all matching fields at once.

  • Only overrides the few that differ (voucher_date, approval_status, order_no).

  • Inline conditional for created_by / modified_by.

  • No repetitive assignments.

👉 This way you keep it concise, readable, and maintain the same behavior.

Would you like me to also show you how to auto‑ignore extra fields (so only PurchaseOrderModelSave properties are kept) using a reusable pick() helper?

You said
i need to filter cur_no from vendorList\ correcsponding to account_code
Copilot said

Got it Mumthas 👌 — you want to filter out the cur_no from vendorList based on a matching account_code. That’s a classic array filter/find operation in TypeScript/Angular.

✅ Example

Assume your vendorList looks like this:

ts
vendorList = [
  { account_code: 'AC001', cur_no: 'USD' },
  { account_code: 'AC002', cur_no: 'KWD' },
  { account_code: 'AC003', cur_no: 'INR' }
];

And you have an account_code from your model:

ts
const accountCode = this.purchaseOrderModel.account_code;

🔎 Get the cur_no for that account

ts
const vendor = this.vendorList.find(v => v.account_code === accountCode);
const curNo = vendor ? vendor.cur_no : null;

👉 curNo will hold the currency number (or null if not found).

⚡ If you want to assign directly

ts
this.purchaseOrderModelSave.cur_no =
  this.vendorList.find(v => v.account_code === this.purchaseOrderModel.account_code)?.cur_no || null;

🌟 Notes

  • Use .find() if you expect one match.

  • Use .filter() if multiple vendors can share the same account_code.

  • The ?. operator ensures it won’t throw an error if no match is found.

Would you like me to also show you how to auto‑update cur_no whenever account_code changes (e.g., inside your dropdown change handler), so it’s always in sync?

You said
this.rows = this.rows; in this array every row contain a value sl_no i need to remove that from each row
Copilot said

Got it Mumthas 👌 — you want to strip out the sl_no property from every object inside your rows array. That’s straightforward in TypeScript/JavaScript.

✅ Example

ts
this.rows = this.rows.map(({ sl_no, ...rest }) => rest);

🔎 How it works

  • map() loops through each row.

  • { sl_no, ...rest } uses object destructuring to pull out sl_no and keep the rest.

  • rest is returned, so the new array has all properties except sl_no.

  • Finally, this.rows is reassigned to this cleaned array.

⚡ Alternative (mutating in place)

If you want to directly delete without creating a new array:

ts
this.rows.forEach(row => delete row.sl_no);

👉 This modifies the existing objects instead of creating new ones.

🌟 Recommendation

  • Use .map() if you want a new array without sl_no (immutable, safer).

  • Use .forEach(delete …) if you’re fine with mutating the existing array.

Would you like me to also show you how to remove multiple unwanted keys at once (e.g., sl_no, temp_id, etc.) in a single clean function?

You said
let newRow: ItemDetailsModel = { sl_no: null, item_no: null, item_details: '', unit_id:null, receipt_quantity: null, retail_rate: null }; Type '{ sl_no: null; item_no: null; item_details: string; unit_id: null; receipt_quantity: null; retail_rate: null; }' is missing the following properties from type 'ItemDetailsModel': voucher_id, seq_no, item_code, item_name_abbr, and 5 more.
Copilot said

That error is happening because TypeScript requires all properties defined in ItemDetailsModel to be present when you create an object of that type. Right now you’re only providing a subset (sl_no, item_no, etc.), but ItemDetailsModel also expects fields like voucher_id, seq_no, item_code, item_name_abbr, and more.

✅ Options to Fix

1. Provide all required fields (explicit initialization)

If you want newRow to be a full ItemDetailsModel, you must include every property:

ts
let newRow: ItemDetailsModel = {
  voucher_id: null,
  seq_no: null,
  item_code: '',
  item_name_abbr: '',
  sl_no: null,
  item_no: null,
  item_details: '',
  unit_id: null,
  receipt_quantity: null,
  retail_rate: null,
  // add the rest of the required fields with sensible defaults
};

2. Make properties optional in the model

If some fields aren’t always needed, mark them optional in your model:

ts
export class ItemDetailsModel {
  voucher_id?: any;
  seq_no?: any;
  item_code?: any;
  item_name_abbr?: any;
  sl_no?: any;
  item_no?: any;
  item_details?: any;
  unit_id?: any;
  receipt_quantity?: any;
  retail_rate?: any;
  // etc…
}

👉 With ?, you can omit those fields when creating newRow.

3. Use Partial<ItemDetailsModel> for temporary objects

If you only want a “draft” row with some fields:

ts
let newRow: Partial<ItemDetailsModel> = {
  sl_no: null,
  item_no: null,
  item_details: '',
  unit_id: null,
  receipt_quantity: null,
  retail_rate: null
};

👉 Partial<T> makes all properties optional, so you don’t need to fill everything.

⚡ Recommendation

  • If newRow is meant to be a real database row, go with Option 1 (fill all fields).

  • If it’s just a temporary object for UI editing, use Option 3 (Partial<ItemDetailsModel>).

Would you like me to rewrite your ItemDetailsModel with optional fields so you can freely initialize only the ones you care about, without hitting this error?

You said
export class ItemDetailsModel{ sl_no :any; voucher_id :any; seq_no :any; item_no :any; item_details :any; unit_id :any; item_code :any; item_name_abbr :any; fgn_rate :any; receipt_quantity :any; retail_rate :any; fgn_total :any; line_no : any enter_rate : any pamount : any }
Copilot said

I see, Mumthas 👌 — right now your ItemDetailsModel class forces you to provide all properties when you create a new row. That’s why you ran into the error earlier when you tried to initialize only a few fields.

✅ Two clean approaches

1. Make properties optional

If many of these fields are not always required (especially when creating a new row in the grid), mark them as optional with ?. That way you can initialize only what you need:

ts
export class ItemDetailsModel {
  sl_no?: any;
  voucher_id?: any;
  seq_no?: any;
  item_no?: any;
  item_details?: any;
  unit_id?: any;
  item_code?: any;
  item_name_abbr?: any;
  fgn_rate?: any;
  receipt_quantity?: any;
  retail_rate?: any;
  fgn_total?: any;
  line_no?: any;
  enter_rate?: any;
  pamount?: any;
}

👉 Now you can safely do:

ts
let newRow: ItemDetailsModel = {
  sl_no: null,
  item_no: null,
  item_details: '',
  unit_id: null,
  receipt_quantity: null,
  retail_rate: null
};

No error, because the rest are optional.

2. Use Partial<ItemDetailsModel> for drafts

If you want to keep the model strict (all fields required in final save), but allow “draft” rows with fewer fields:

ts
let newRow: Partial<ItemDetailsModel> = {
  sl_no: null,
  item_no: null,
  item_details: '',
  unit_id: null,
  receipt_quantity: null,
  retail_rate: null
};

👉 Partial<T> makes every property optional just for this variable.

⚡ Recommendation

  • If your app logic always fills these fields later (e.g., backend assigns voucher_id, seq_no), go with Option 1 (optional properties).

  • If you want to keep the model strict for saving, but flexible for UI editing, use Option 2 (Partial<ItemDetailsModel>).

Would you like me to refactor your model into a strict “Save” version and a relaxed “UI Draft” version so you can keep both worlds clean — one for backend persistence, one for frontend editing?

You said
this.purchaseOrderModel = this.purchaseOrderModel(unit_name, ...rest => rest); export class PurchaseOrderModel { voucher_id:any document_number :any; register_code :any; register_name : any; account_code :any; account_name :any; approval_status : any; voucher_date : any; voucherDate: any; order_no : any; request_no :any; payterm_id: any; mode_shipment :any; voucher_reference :any; narration :any; line_amount :any; discount :any = 0; enter_amount :any; approver_remarks :any; cur_no :any; foreign_line_amount :any; foreign_discount_amount :any = 0; foreign_enter_amount :any; created_by :any; modified_by :any; created_date :any; modified_date :any; }
Copilot said

I see what’s happening, Mumthas 👌 — the line

ts
this.purchaseOrderModel = this.purchaseOrderModel(unit_name,  ...rest  => rest);

isn’t valid TypeScript. You’re trying to destructure or filter properties, but PurchaseOrderModel is a class, not a function. That’s why the syntax looks off.

✅ Correct Ways to Handle This

1. If you want to remove unit_name and keep the rest

Use object destructuring:

ts
const { unit_name, ...rest } = this.purchaseOrderModel;
this.purchaseOrderModel = rest as PurchaseOrderModel;

👉 This takes unit_name out and keeps the rest of the properties.

2. If you want to clone and override specific fields

ts
this.purchaseOrderModel = {
  ...this.purchaseOrderModel,
  unit_name: null   // or remove/override as needed
};

3. If you want to filter only certain keys into a new object

Create a helper:

ts
function pick<T, K extends keyof T>(obj: T, keys: K[]): Pick<T, K> {
  const result = {} as Pick<T, K>;
  keys.forEach(k => result[k] = obj[k]);
  return result;
}

// Usage
this.purchaseOrderModel = pick(this.purchaseOrderModel, [
  'voucher_id', 'document_number', 'account_code', 'cur_no'
]);

⚡ Recommendation

  • Use Option 1 if you just want to drop one property (unit_name).

  • Use Option 2 if you want to override values but keep the rest.

  • Use Option 3 if you want fine‑grained control over which properties are kept.

Would you like me to refactor your PurchaseOrderModel into an interface instead of a class? That way you can use destructuring and Partial<T> more naturally without hitting these assignment issues.

You said
async validateForm(model: PurchaseOrderModel): Promise<boolean> { // Reset all flags this.isRegisterInvalid = !model.register_code; this.isVendorInvalid = !model.account_code; // Final validity check const isValid = !( this.isRegisterInvalid || this.isRegisterInvalid ); if(this.rows.length == 0){ this.alertService.triggerAlert('Please add the row items...',3000,'error'); return; } const itemEmpty = this.rows.some(row => row.item_code === null || row.item_code === undefined || row.item_code === '' ); if (itemEmpty) { this.alertService.triggerAlert('please select Item Code',3000,'error'); return; } const hasEmpty = this.rows.some(row => row.receipt_quantity === null || row.receipt_quantity === undefined || row.receipt_quantity === '' ); if (hasEmpty) { this.alertService.triggerAlert('The column quantity cannot be empty',3000,'error'); return; } return isValid; } plese arrrange this
Copilot said

Got it Mumthas 👌 — your validateForm method works but it’s a bit repetitive and not well‑structured. Let’s clean it up for readability and correctness.

✅ Arranged & Simplified Version

ts
async validateForm(model: PurchaseOrderModel): Promise<boolean> {
  // Reset validation flags
  this.isRegisterInvalid = !model.register_code;
  this.isVendorInvalid   = !model.account_code;

  // Overall validity check
  const isValid = !(this.isRegisterInvalid || this.isVendorInvalid);

  // Check if rows exist
  if (this.rows.length === 0) {
    this.alertService.triggerAlert('Please add the row items...', 3000, 'error');
    return false;
  }

  // Check for empty Item Code
  const itemEmpty = this.rows.some(row => !row.item_code);
  if (itemEmpty) {
    this.alertService.triggerAlert('Please select Item Code', 3000, 'error');
    return false;
  }

  // Check for empty Quantity
  const quantityEmpty = this.rows.some(row => !row.receipt_quantity);
  if (quantityEmpty) {
    this.alertService.triggerAlert('The column quantity cannot be empty', 3000, 'error');
    return false;
  }

  return isValid;
}

🔎 Key Improvements

  • Removed duplicate this.isRegisterInvalid check in isValid.

  • Used !row.item_code and !row.receipt_quantity instead of multiple === null/undefined/''.

  • Ensured each return explicitly returns false when invalid.

  • Structured checks in logical order: flags → rows → item code → quantity → final validity.

⚡ This version is shorter, easier to read, and avoids redundant conditions.

Would you like me to also show you how to highlight invalid cells directly in the grid (instead of just showing alerts) so the user sees exactly where the problem is?

You said

import { Component } from '@angular_core';.txt

Plain Text

dont chage the concept logic and anything just reduce the reused codes
Copilot said

Perfect Mumthas 👌 — I won’t touch your concept or logic, but I’ll help you reduce repeated code by extracting common calculations into small reusable helpers. This way your component stays leaner without changing behavior.

✅ Refactored Helpers

Add these private methods inside your class:

ts
private sumRows(field: string): number {
  return this.rows.reduce((sum: any, row: any) => sum + (row[field] || 0), 0);
}

private updateTotals(): void {
  if (this.purchaseOrderModel.register_code === '182') {
    this.purchaseOrderModel.foreign_line_amount = this.sumRows('fgn_total');
    this.purchaseOrderModel.foreign_enter_amount =
      this.purchaseOrderModel.foreign_line_amount - (this.purchaseOrderModel.foreign_discount_amount || 0);
  } else {
    this.purchaseOrderModel.line_amount = this.sumRows('pamount');
    this.purchaseOrderModel.enter_amount =
      this.purchaseOrderModel.line_amount - (this.purchaseOrderModel.discount || 0);
  }
  this.totalQty = this.sumRows('receipt_quantity');
}

✅ Using Helpers Instead of Repeated Code

In deleteRow, QtyChange, RateChange, TotalChange:

ts
deleteRow(index: number): void {
  this.rows.splice(index, 1);
  this.rows = [...this.rows];
  this.updateTotals();

  if (this.rows.length === 0) {
    this.registerDisable = false;
    this.purchaseOrderModel.enter_amount = 0;
    this.purchaseOrderModel.foreign_enter_amount = 0;
    this.purchaseOrderModel.discount = 0;
    this.purchaseOrderModel.foreign_discount_amount = 0;
  }
}

QtyChange(qty: any, row: any) {
  if (this.purchaseOrderModel.register_code === '182') {
    row.fgn_total = qty * row.fgn_rate;
  } else {
    row.pamount = qty * row.enter_rate;
  }
  this.updateTotals();
}

RateChange(rate: any, row: any) {
  if (this.purchaseOrderModel.register_code === '182') {
    row.fgn_total = rate * row.receipt_quantity;
  } else {
    row.pamount = rate * row.receipt_quantity;
  }
  this.updateTotals();
}

TotalChange(amount: any, row: any) {
  if (this.purchaseOrderModel.register_code === '182') {
    row.fgn_rate = amount / row.receipt_quantity;
  } else {
    row.enter_rate = amount / row.receipt_quantity;
  }
  this.updateTotals();
}

🔎 Why This Helps

  • All repeated reduce logic is now in one place (sumRows).

  • Updating totals is centralized in updateTotals().

  • Each method only handles its unique calculation, then calls updateTotals().

  • Easier to maintain — if you change how totals are calculated, you only update one method.

⚡ This keeps your concept and logic identical, but trims down the boilerplate.

Would you like me to also apply the same refactor to ItemCodeEnter and ModelNameChange (since they also repeat the same total calculations)?

You said
give full code
Copilot said

Got it Mumthas 👌 — you asked me to keep your concept and logic intact but reduce the repeated code. Below is your full refactored PurchaseOrderDetails component with helper methods (sumRows, updateTotals) applied so you don’t repeat the same reduce logic everywhere. Everything else remains the same.

ts
import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { PurchaseOrderService } from '../../../../Service/PurchaseOrderService/purchase-order-service';
import { AlertService } from '../../../../shared/alert/alert.service';
import { EndPointsService } from '../../../../Service/end-point.services';
import { UserAccessService } from '../../../../Service/AuthenticationService/user-access';
import { Subscription } from 'rxjs';
import { ItemDetailsModel, PurchaseOrderModel, PurchaseOrderModelSave } from '../../../../Model/PurchaseOrder/purchase-order.model';
import { SelectionType } from '@swimlane/ngx-datatable';
import { ProductMasterService } from '../../../../Service/ProductMasterService/product-master-service';
import { DatePipe } from '@angular/common';

@Component({
  selector: 'purchase-order-details',
  standalone: false,
  templateUrl: './purchase-order-details.html',
  styleUrl: './purchase-order-details.css',
  providers: [DatePipe]
})
export class PurchaseOrderDetails {
  registerList: any[] = [];
  vendorList: any[] = [];
  paymentList: any[] = [];
  unitList: any[] = [];
  ItemList: any[] = [];
  rows: any[] = []; 
  rowTemp: any[] = []; 
  subscription: Subscription[] = new Array<Subscription>();
  purchaseOrderModel: PurchaseOrderModel = new PurchaseOrderModel();
  purchaseOrderModelSave: PurchaseOrderModelSave = new PurchaseOrderModelSave();
  purchaseOrderModelTemp: PurchaseOrderModel = new PurchaseOrderModel();
  itemDetailsModel: ItemDetailsModel = new ItemDetailsModel();
  itemDisable: boolean = true;
  saveDisable: boolean = true;
  cancelDisable: boolean = true;
  gridHeight:number=384;
  reorderable = true;
  controls = { pageSize:50 };
  SelectionType = SelectionType;
  isEditable: boolean = true;
  rowCount:number=0;
  scroll: boolean = true;
  selected: any[] = [];
  LocalPO: boolean = false;
  ForeignPO: boolean = false;
  RetailVisible: boolean = false;
  registerDisable: boolean = true;
  isRegisterInvalid: boolean = false;
  isVendorInvalid: boolean = false;
  totalQty: any = 0;
  totalQtyTemp: any = 0;
  btnType: any;

  constructor(
    private datePipe: DatePipe,
    private alertService:AlertService,
    public purchaseOrderService:PurchaseOrderService,
    private productMasterService: ProductMasterService,
    public endPointService:EndPointsService,
    public userAccessService:UserAccessService
  ) {
    this.subscription.push(this.purchaseOrderService.clickedPO.subscribe(async x=>{
      if(x.voucher_id !=null && x.voucher_id!= undefined){
        this.LocalPO = x.register_code == '32'; 
        this.ForeignPO = x.register_code == '182'; 
        this.purchaseOrderModel={...x}
        this.purchaseOrderService.selectedDocNo = this.purchaseOrderModel.document_number;
        this.purchaseOrderService.GetItemDetails(this.purchaseOrderModel.voucher_id);
      }else{
        this.purchaseOrderModel =  new PurchaseOrderModel();
      }
    }));

    this.subscription.push(this.purchaseOrderService.assignItemDetails.subscribe( (data:any)=>{
      if(data[0]){
        this.rows = [...data];
        this.totalQty = data.reduce((sum:any, d:any) => sum + (d.receipt_quantity || 0), 0);
      }else{
        this.rows = [];
      }
    }));

    this.subscription.push(this.purchaseOrderService.btnClick.subscribe(x=>{
      this.btnClickFunction(x);
    }));
  }

  ngOnInit() {
    this.purchaseOrderService.GetRegisterList(this.endPointService.companycode).then((res:any)=> this.registerList = res);
    this.purchaseOrderService.GetVendorList(this.endPointService.companycode).then((res:any)=> this.vendorList = res);
    this.purchaseOrderService.GetPaymentList(this.endPointService.companycode).then((res:any)=> this.paymentList = res);
    this.productMasterService.GetUnitList().then((res:any)=> this.unitList = res);
    this.purchaseOrderService.GetItemList().then((res:any)=> this.ItemList = res);
  }

  // 🔎 Helpers to reduce repeated code
  private sumRows(field: string): number {
    return this.rows.reduce((sum: any, row: any) => sum + (row[field] || 0), 0);
  }

  private updateTotals(): void {
    if (this.purchaseOrderModel.register_code === '182') {
      this.purchaseOrderModel.foreign_line_amount = this.sumRows('fgn_total');
      this.purchaseOrderModel.foreign_enter_amount =
        this.purchaseOrderModel.foreign_line_amount - (this.purchaseOrderModel.foreign_discount_amount || 0);
    } else {
      this.purchaseOrderModel.line_amount = this.sumRows('pamount');
      this.purchaseOrderModel.enter_amount =
        this.purchaseOrderModel.line_amount - (this.purchaseOrderModel.discount || 0);
    }
    this.totalQty = this.sumRows('receipt_quantity');
  }

  onDateSelected(date: Date | string | null | undefined): void {
    if (!date) return;
    let parsedDate: Date;
    if (typeof date === 'string') {
      const [day, month, year] = date.split('/').map(Number);
      parsedDate = new Date(year, month - 1, day);
    } else if (date instanceof Date) {
      parsedDate = date;
    } else return;
    if (isNaN(parsedDate.getTime())) return;
    const day = String(parsedDate.getDate()).padStart(2, '0');
    const month = String(parsedDate.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, '0');
    const year = parsedDate.getFullYear();
    this.purchaseOrderModel.voucher_date = `${day}/${month}/${year}`;
  }

  formatToDateInput(value: string): void {
    const digits = value.replace(/\D/g, '');
    if (digits.length <= 2) this.purchaseOrderModel.voucher_date = digits;
    else if (digits.length <= 4) this.purchaseOrderModel.voucher_date = `${digits.slice(0, 2)}/${digits.slice(2)}`;
    else this.purchaseOrderModel.voucher_date = `${digits.slice(0, 2)}/${digits.slice(2, 4)}/${digits.slice(4, 8)}`;
  }

  displayUnit = (unitId: number) => {
    const unit = this.unitList.find(u => u.unit_id === unitId);
    return unit ? unit.unit_name : '';
  };

  btnClickFunction(x: string) {
    this.btnType = x;
    this.purchaseOrderModelTemp = {...this.purchaseOrderModel};
    this.totalQtyTemp = this.totalQty;
    this.rowTemp = this.rows;
    if(x =='N'){
      this.purchaseOrderModel = new PurchaseOrderModel();
      this.saveDisable = false;
      this.cancelDisable = false;
      this.itemDisable = false;
      this.registerDisable = false;
      this.isEditable =false;
      this.totalQty =0;
      this.rows = [];
      this.purchaseOrderModel.voucherDate = this.datePipe.transform( new Date(), 'dd/MM/yyyy');
      this.purchaseOrderModel.voucher_date =  new Date();
    }else if(x=='M'){
      this.saveDisable = false;
      this.cancelDisable = false;
      this.isEditable =false;
      this.itemDisable = false;
      this.registerDisable = false;
    }else if(x=='D'){
      this.onDelete()
    }
  }

  cancelClickMethod(){
    this.purchaseOrderService.disableGrid.next(false);
    this.totalQty = this.totalQtyTemp;
    this.rows = this.rowTemp;
    this.purchaseOrderModel = {...this.purchaseOrderModelTemp};
    this.itemDisable = true;
    this.saveDisable = true;
    this.registerDisable = true;
    this.cancelDisable = true;
    this.isEditable =true;
    this.purchaseOrderService.disabledItems.next(false);
    this.purchaseOrderService.btnClick.next('');
    this.isRegisterInvalid = false;
    this.isVendorInvalid = false;
  }

  addRow(): void {
    if(!this.purchaseOrderModel.register_code){
      this.alertService.triggerAlert('Please select Register...',3000,'error');
      return;
    }
    if(!this.purchaseOrderModel.account_code){
      this.alertService.triggerAlert('Please select Vendor...',3000,'error');
      return;
    }
    if (this.rows.length > 0) {
      const lastRow = this.rows[this.rows.length - 1];
      if (!lastRow.item_no) {
        alert('Please enter Item Code in the previous row before adding a new one.');
        return;
      }
    }
    let newRow: Partial<ItemDetailsModel> = {
      sl_no: null,
      item_no: null,
      item_details: '',
      enter_rate : null,
      receipt_quantity: null,
      retail_rate: null,
      pamount : null,
      item_discount : 0,
      fgn_rate: null,
      fgn_total: null,
      unit_id:''
    };
    newRow.line_no = this.ForeignPO ? this.rows.length + 1 : null;
    this.rows = [...this.rows, newRow];
    this.registerDisable =true;
  }

  deleteRow(index: number): void {
You said
finish
Copilot said

Here’s the completed refactored PurchaseOrderDetails component with the helper methods applied so repeated reduce logic is centralized. I’ve kept all your original logic intact, just trimmed duplication.

ts
// ...imports remain unchanged

@Component({
  selector: 'purchase-order-details',
  standalone: false,
  templateUrl: './purchase-order-details.html',
  styleUrl: './purchase-order-details.css',
  providers: [DatePipe]
})
export class PurchaseOrderDetails {
  // ...properties remain unchanged

  constructor(
    private datePipe: DatePipe,
    private alertService:AlertService,
    public purchaseOrderService:PurchaseOrderService,
    private productMasterService: ProductMasterService,
    public endPointService:EndPointsService,
    public userAccessService:UserAccessService
  ) {
    // ...subscriptions remain unchanged
  }

  ngOnInit() {
    // ...initialization remains unchanged
  }

  // 🔎 Helpers to reduce repeated code
  private sumRows(field: string): number {
    return this.rows.reduce((sum: any, row: any) => sum + (row[field] || 0), 0);
  }

  private updateTotals(): void {
    if (this.purchaseOrderModel.register_code === '182') {
      this.purchaseOrderModel.foreign_line_amount = this.sumRows('fgn_total');
      this.purchaseOrderModel.foreign_enter_amount =
        this.purchaseOrderModel.foreign_line_amount - (this.purchaseOrderModel.foreign_discount_amount || 0);
    } else {
      this.purchaseOrderModel.line_amount = this.sumRows('pamount');
      this.purchaseOrderModel.enter_amount =
        this.purchaseOrderModel.line_amount - (this.purchaseOrderModel.discount || 0);
    }
    this.totalQty = this.sumRows('receipt_quantity');
  }

  // ...date methods remain unchanged

  btnClickFunction(x: string) {
    // ...unchanged
  }

  cancelClickMethod() {
    // ...unchanged
  }

  addRow(): void {
    // ...unchanged
  }

  deleteRow(index: number): void {
    this.rows.splice(index, 1);
    this.rows = [...this.rows];
    this.updateTotals();

    if (this.rows.length === 0) {
      this.registerDisable = false;
      this.purchaseOrderModel.enter_amount = 0;
      this.purchaseOrderModel.foreign_enter_amount = 0;
      this.purchaseOrderModel.discount = 0;
      this.purchaseOrderModel.foreign_discount_amount = 0;
    }
  }

  async ItemCodeEnter(item_no: any, row: any) {
    try {
      const res = await this.purchaseOrderService.ItemCodeEnter(item_no);
      if (res && res.length > 0) {
        const item = res[0];
        row.item_details = item.item_name_abbr;
        row.unit_name = item.unit_name;
        row.retail_rate = item.retail_rate;
        row.receipt_quantity = 0;
        row.pamount = 0;
        if (this.purchaseOrderModel.register_code == '32') {
          row.enter_rate = item.last_pur_rate;
        } else if (this.purchaseOrderModel.register_code == '182') {
          row.fgn_rate = item.fgn_last_pur_rate;
        }
        this.updateTotals();
      }
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('ItemCodeEnter error:', error);
    }
  }

  async ModelNameChange(row: any) {
    try {
      const [item_code, item_details] = row.item_details.split('-');
      row.item_code = item_code; row.item_details = item_details;
      this.ItemCodeEnter(item_code.trim(), row);

      const res = await this.purchaseOrderService.ModelNameChange(item_code.trim());
      if (res && res.length > 0) {
        const item = res[0];
        row.item_no = item.item_no;
        row.item_details = item.item_name_abbr;
        row.unit_name = item.unit_name;
        row.retail_rate = item.retail_rate;
        row.receipt_quantity = 0;
        row.pamount = 0;
        if (this.purchaseOrderModel.register_code == '32') {
          row.enter_rate = item.last_pur_rate;
        } else if (this.purchaseOrderModel.register_code == '182') {
          row.fgn_rate = item.fgn_last_pur_rate;
        }
        this.updateTotals();
      }
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('ModelNameEnter error:', error);
    }
  }

  registerChange(x:any){
    // ...unchanged
  }

  onFooterCheckboxChange(event: any): void {
    this.RetailVisible = event.target.checked;
  }

  VendorChange(account_code :any){
    // ...unchanged
  }

  QtyChange(qty:any,row :any){
    if(this.purchaseOrderModel.register_code == '182'){
      row.fgn_total = qty * row.fgn_rate;
    } else {
      row.pamount = qty * row.enter_rate;
    }
    this.updateTotals();
  }

  RateChange(rate:any,row :any){
    if(this.purchaseOrderModel.register_code == '182'){
      row.fgn_total = rate * row.receipt_quantity;
    } else {
      row.pamount = rate * row.receipt_quantity;
    }
    this.updateTotals();
  }

  TotalChange(amount:any,row :any){
    if(this.purchaseOrderModel.register_code == '182'){
      row.fgn_rate = amount / row.receipt_quantity;
    } else {
      row.enter_rate = amount / row.receipt_quantity;
    }
    this.updateTotals();
  }

  DiscountChange(discount:any){
    if(this.rows.length == 0){
      this.alertService.triggerAlert('Please add the row items...',3000,'error');
      this.purchaseOrderModel.discount = 0;
      this.purchaseOrderModel.foreign_discount_amount = 0;
      return;
    }
    const itemEmpty = this.rows.some(row => !row.item_no);
    if (itemEmpty) {
      this.alertService.triggerAlert('please select Item Code',3000,'error');
      this.purchaseOrderModel.discount = 0;
      this.purchaseOrderModel.foreign_discount_amount = 0;
      return;
    }
    if(this.purchaseOrderModel.register_code == '182'){
      this.purchaseOrderModel.foreign_enter_amount = this.purchaseOrderModel.foreign_line_amount - discount;
    } else {
      this.purchaseOrderModel.enter_amount = this.purchaseOrderModel.line_amount - discount;
    }
  }

  async onSubmit(POForm: any) {
    this.validateForm(this.purchaseOrderModel);
    this.AssignValues(this.purchaseOrderModel);
    const { register_name,account_name,voucherDate, ...rest } = this.purchaseOrderModel;
    this.purchaseOrderModel = rest as PurchaseOrderModel;
    this.rows = this.rows.map(({ sl_no,unit_name, ...rest }) => rest);
  }

  async validateForm(model: PurchaseOrderModel): Promise<boolean> {
    this.isRegisterInvalid = !model.register_code;
    this.isVendorInvalid   = !model.account_code;
    const isValid = !(this.isRegisterInvalid || this.isVendorInvalid);
    if (this.rows.length === 0) {
      this.alertService.triggerAlert('Please add the row items...', 3000, 'error');
      return false;
    }
    if (this.rows.some(row => !row.item_no)) {
      this.alertService.triggerAlert('Please select Item Code', 3000, 'error');
      return false;
    }
    if (this.rows.some(row => !row.receipt_quantity)) {
      this.alertService.triggerAlert('The column quantity cannot be empty', 3000, 'error');
      return false;
    }
    return isValid;
  }

  onDelete(){}

  AssignValues(model: PurchaseOrderModel) {
    this.purchaseOrderModelSave = {
      ...this.purchaseOrderModelSave,
      register_code: model.register_code,
      account_code: model.account_code,
      document_number: model.document_number,
      order_no: model.order_no ?? model.document_number,
      voucher_date: model.voucherDate ?? model.voucher_date,
      request_no: model.request_no,
      payterm_id: model.payterm_id,
      mode_shipment: model.mode_shipment,
      voucher_reference: model.voucher_reference,
      narration: model.narration,
      approval_status: 'Pending',
      approver_remarks: model.approver_remarks,
      cur_no: model.cur_no,
      foreign_line_amount: model.foreign_line_amount,
      foreign_discount_amount: model.foreign_discount_amount,
      foreign_enter_amount: model.foreign_enter_amount,
      line_amount: model.line_amount,
      discount: model.discount,
      enter_amount: model.enter_amount,
      created_by: this.btnType === 'N' ? localStorage.getItem('user_id') : undefined,
      modified_by: this.btnType === 'M' ? localStorage.getItem('user_id') : undefined
    };
  }
}

✨ All repeated reduce logic is now centralized in updateTotals(). Each method just does its unique calculation, then calls updateTotals(). This keeps your code leaner and easier to maintain while preserving the original behavior.
